title: Living in University City, MO — Complete 2026 Guide
content:
University City is one of St. Louis's most vibrant inner-ring suburbs, 15 minutes west of Downtown. Combines walkable commercial areas, diverse housing, and a thriving arts scene centered around Washington University. Population density: 5,967/sq mi.

The Delmar Loop: U-City's defining feature — lively commercial corridor along Delmar Blvd with restaurants, music venues, theaters, locally-owned shops. Cultural heart of the neighborhood.

Neighborhoods:
- Parkview Gardens: Steps from Loop, early 1900s homes (bungalows, Tudor, Georgian Revival). $300K condos to $600K-$800K homes. Historic District.
- University Heights No. 1: Early 1900s planned subdivision, National Register of Historic Places. Tudor Revival, Arts & Crafts, Queen Anne. $500K-$1M+.
- North Central: Mid-century bungalows and ranches (1940s-60s). $100K-$350K. Quiet, dog-friendly.
- University Hills: Larger historic homes 1920s-30s near WashU. Mid-$300Ks to $1M+. Active subdivision association.
- Midland Core: Early 1900s-1930s homes near Loop. $135K-$300K+. Walkable yet quiet.
- Blackberry/Village of University Place: 1930s-40s historic + 2000s-2020s infill. $400K-$500K typical, some into millions.

Median Home Price (2026): $364,000. Cost of living ~7% below national average.

Schools: University City School District. Julia Goldstein Early Childhood Center, Flynn Park/Jackson Park/Pershing/Barbara Jordan Elementary. Brittany Woods Middle School. University City High School (~815 students, AP and TAG programs).

Dining: Salt + Smoke (BBQ), Nudo House (ramen), Taco Buddha, Ranoush (Middle Eastern), Nobu's (sushi). Iconic spots: Fitz's Root Beer (est. 1947, vintage bottling line), Blueberry Hill (where Chuck Berry performed monthly until 2014).

Landmarks: Chuck Berry Statue, St. Louis Walk of Fame, Moonrise Hotel (rooftop bar), Loop Trolley (free, May-Oct). Music venues: The Pageant, COCA, Tivoli Theatre, The Duck Room, 560 Music Center.

Parks: Heman Park (85 acres, summer concerts, farmers market, pool, sports). Centennial Commons recreation center. Ruth Park Golf Course (est. 1931, 9-hole). U-City Dog Park. Centennial Greenway trail through Forest Park to Loop.

Development: Market at Olive (50-acre hub, Costco open, Target and Dierbergs under construction). LOCAL on Delmar (259 units, summer 2026, rooftop pool).

Character: Dense, eclectic college-town meets city. Block-to-block variation. Mix of longtime residents, students, artists, professionals. Washington University shapes much of the culture.
